page: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.large is a general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the Nitro hypervisor, includes no local storage, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The instance achieves strong single-core metrics, placing in the top 25% for Geekbench single-core scores, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth performance are significantly limited. Stress-ng multi-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 10%, and memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks is categorized as weak to poor. Similarly, Passmark CPU and memory scores, database operations, and Redis SET operations rank in the bottom 25%.

The hardware profile presents a qualitative tradeoff: high single-core processing speeds and advanced instruction sets, such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX, are paired with limited multi-core scaling and low memory bandwidth. This makes the instance cost-efficient for workloads that do not require heavy parallelization or high memory throughput. It is appropriate for single-threaded applications, basic text processing, and lightweight web serving, but is less optimal for multi-threaded compilation, database hosting, or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.large is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 2 vCPUs, 1 physical core, and 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance lacks local storage and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate a strong showing in single-core tasks, with top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench single-core tests. Conversely, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are weak, with stress-ng multi-core and large-block memory reads placing in the bottom 10%. This resource profile represents a qualitative tradeoff, offering cost-efficient single-core execution at the expense of parallel processing power. It is recommended for single-threaded applications, text processing, and lightweight utility workloads rather than intensive database operations or multi-threaded software compilation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.large is a general-purpose instance with an Intel Xeon CPU, 2 vCPUs, and 8 GB of DDR5 RAM, delivering strong single-core performance but limited multi-core and memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.large is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8 GB DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: An x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine delivering strong single-threaded performance and DDR5 memory for lightweight, compute-focused workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM
Strong single-core performance in Geekbench and single-threaded compression
Built on the Nitro hypervisor with dedicated CPU allocation
Limited multi-core scaling and low memory bandwidth under load
No local storage and 0 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Appropriate for single-threaded tasks, text processing, and basic utility workloads
